perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose . As used herein, _______________________ and NU shall include, individually and collectively, their respective officers, directors , consultants, employees and associated researchers , provided, however, that NU shall not include its students.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall use the Data provided by the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose . As used herein, _______________________ and NU shall include, individually and collectively, their respective officers, directors , consultants, employees and associated researchers , provided, however, that NU shall not include its students.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Data' in the first sentence of Section 2 creates ambiguity. 'Confidential Information' is a defined term, while 'Data' is not. This leads to uncertainty about whether the restrictions in the agreement apply only to 'Confidential Information' as defined in Section 1, or to a broader, undefined category of 'Data', potentially invalidating the confidentiality provision. This creates an in-text contradiction because 'Data' and 'Confidential Information' are used in the same phrase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purpose . The Recipient shall use the Data provided by the Disclosing Party solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose . As used herein, _______________________ and NU shall include, individually and collectively, their respective officers, directors , consultants, employees and associated researchers , provided, however, that NU shall not include its students.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the defined term 'Confidential Information'. The changed text replaces this with the undefined term 'Data'. The contradicted text uses 'Data'. Since 'Data' is not a defined term, it creates ambiguity as to whether the clause only applies to 'Confidential Information', or to a broader undefined set of data. This creates an in-text contradiction because the meaning and scope of the obligation are unclear and 'Data' and 'Confidential Information' are used seemingly interchangeably without definition as per the Misaligned Terminology definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall not disclose the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall not disclose the Sensitive Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By using 'Sensitive Information' instead of 'Confidential Information' in the first clause of Section 3, while retaining 'Confidential Information' in the rest of the section, a contradiction is created. It's unclear whether the non-disclosure obligation extends only to the defined 'Confidential Information' or also to some other, undefined 'Sensitive Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty . The Recipient shall not disclose the Sensitive Information of the Disclosing Party to any third party nor access or use such Confi dential Information other than solely for the Purpose . In all cases, t he Recipient shall use reasonable measures that are at least as stringent as those used by the Recipient to protect its own information of a similar nature (and in no event shall the Re cipient use less than reasonable measures) to keep confidential and safeguard from theft loss and/or unpermitted uses the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in the first clause of Section 3, while retaining 'Confidential Information' in the rest of the section. The contradicted text also reflects the changed text, replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in the first clause but keeping 'Confidential Information' later on. This creates an ambiguity, making it unclear whether the non-disclosure obligation extends only to the defined 'Confidential Information' or also to some other, undefined 'Sensitive Information', directly conflicting within the same section. This meets the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient affirms and agrees that all right s, titles and interests in and to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party are and shall remain at all times the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement or the disclosure of Confidential Information expressly or implicitly grants or confers on Recipie nt any right, title or interest in or to the Confidential Information or other property of the Disclosing Party other than the limited right s to receive , access, possess and use such information solely for the Purpose in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient affirms and agrees that all right s, titles and interests in and to the Trade Secrets of the Disclosing Party are and shall remain at all times the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement or the disclosure of Confidential Information expressly or implicitly grants or confers on Recipie nt any right, title or interest in or to the Confidential Information or other property of the Disclosing Party other than the limited right s to receive , access, possess and use such information solely for the Purpose in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Trade Secrets' in the first sentence of Section 6, while continuing to use 'Confidential Information' throughout the rest of the section, creates a conflict. The Agreement defines 'Confidential Information' but does not define 'Trade Secrets', leading to uncertainty about the ownership of information that may fall into one category but not the other. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding ownership and disclosure rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership . Recipient affirms and agrees that all right s, titles and interests in and to the Trade Secrets of the Disclosing Party are and shall remain at all times the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement or the disclosure of Confidential Information expressly or implicitly grants or confers on Recipie nt any right, title or interest in or to the Confidential Information or other property of the Disclosing Party other than the limited right s to receive , access, possess and use such information solely for the Purpose in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' and the changed text replaces this with 'Trade Secrets' in the first sentence, while continuing to use 'Confidential Information' throughout the rest of the section. The explanation states that this creates a conflict because 'Confidential Information' is defined, but 'Trade Secrets' is not, leading to uncertainty. The contradicted text also uses both terms, highlighting the potential for confusion. This represents a strong in-text contradiction as it directly alters the scope of what is covered, thus breaking compatibility and impacting obligations related to ownership and usage of information.

perturbation.original_text: Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Recipient shall cease use of all Confidential Information furnished hereunder and shall, upon written direction of the Disclosing Party , return to the Disclosing Party , or destroy, all such Confidential Information, together with all copies made thereof by the Recipient . Upon request, the Recipient shall send the Disclosing Party a certificate con firming the destruction or return of all Confidential Information delivered hereunder. Notwithstanding the other provisions of this Section , Recipient may retain one copy of such Confidential Information for archival purposes only and such Confidential Information shall remain subject to this Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement ,.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Recipient shall cease use of all Material furnished hereunder and shall, upon written direction of the Disclosing Party , return to the Disclosing Party , or destroy, all such Confidential Information, together with all copies made thereof by the Recipient . Upon request, the Recipient shall send the Disclosing Party a certificate con firming the destruction or return of all Confidential Information delivered hereunder. Notwithstanding the other provisions of this Section , Recipient may retain one copy of such Confidential Information for archival purposes only and such Confidential Information shall remain subject to this Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement ,.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Material' in the first sentence of Section 14 creates a conflict. The defined term is 'Confidential Information', but the instruction now states to stop using all 'Material' which is undefined, whilst the rest of the section focuses on the defined 'Confidential Information' for return and destruction, and is subject to the terms of the agreement. What about the use of material. The ambiguity creates uncertainty about the scope of obligations upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Effect of Expiration or Termination . Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Recipient shall cease use of all Material furnished hereunder and shall, upon written direction of the Disclosing Party , return to the Disclosing Party , or destroy, all such Confidential Information, together with all copies made thereof by the Recipient . Upon request, the Recipient shall send the Disclosing Party a certificate con firming the destruction or return of all Confidential Information delivered hereunder. Notwithstanding the other provisions of this Section , Recipient may retain one copy of such Confidential Information for archival purposes only and such Confidential Information shall remain subject to this Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement ,.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Material' in the first sentence of the changed text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which refers to 'Confidential Information' for return and destruction obligations. This misalignment of terminology, specifically the introduction of an undefined term 'Material,' creates ambiguity and inconsistency regarding the scope of obligations upon termination, which fits the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.'
